recent_calls.summary: Jennifer called to inquire about the timing of her service appointment and specifically when Patrick would arrive. The agent confirmed Jennifer's identity and informed her that they could not confirm technician arrival times but would pass her request to the team for follow-up. The call ended with the agent assuring a follow-up would be made.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Arturo called to discuss an estimate for cooling service at his condo, referred by his home warranty company, and wanted to know if the estimate was covered by the warranty. The agent confirmed details and informed Arturo that someone from Budget would contact him shortly. Arturo declined information about maintenance programs and did not provide feedback at this time.
The user requested to speak with Amy, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate person. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to follow up on a recent heating service visit, specifically regarding the blower motor repair, and wanted to get in touch with the technician who came to their house. The agent gathered necessary details and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user expressed frustration about difficulty reaching the technician but was seeking resolution.
The user called to speak with Amy and was promptly transferred to a live agent who could assist. The call lasted 16 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

Kevin called to schedule a routine AC maintenance appointment and was provisionally booked for Tuesday, April 14th at 10:00 a.m. The agent confirmed the details and informed Kevin about the standard dispatch fee. Kevin declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user reported an issue with their air conditioning unit not cooling properly and not turning off. They requested a technician visit today around 1 PM, provided contact numbers, and declined additional information about maintenance programs. The call ended with the user providing positive feedback about the customer service experience.

The user called to report an emergency issue with their air conditioner that intermittently stops working and causes the modem to overheat. The agent collected all necessary information, including the user's dispatch number and preferred service day, and confirmed the details. The user declined information about maintenance programs but provided positive feedback on the call experience.
The user called to speak with Nick Marchesano and requested to be transferred to him. The agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate contact.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called Budget to inquire about a service request related to a furnace and concerns about uncovered costs despite their home warranty coverage. The agent gathered necessary details and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance. The user requested a follow-up as soon as possible.

Jamie called to inquire about the arrival time of a service technician. The agent was unable to provide exact arrival times and attempted to find an appointment but found none. Jamie requested to speak to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to a representative.

The user called to reschedule a service appointment for a non-working electrical outlet. The agent confirmed the details and the user's ownership of the property, then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Levy called to schedule the installation of a flue cap part related to a new air conditioner and furnace setup under an American Home Shield warranty. The agent confirmed the service details and scheduled the installation for Monday morning. The call ended with the agent noting the information for follow-up and the user confirming no changes were needed.

The user called to inquire about a canceled service dispatch for an AC unit that is not working. The agent collected all necessary details and confirmed the information with the user. The user declined information about maintenance programs and was informed that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up soon.

The user called to inquire about their heating and cooling system, mentioning it was bought about six years ago. The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred to another agent.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing herself and asking how she could help, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user briefly mentioned leaving the door open, and the agent attempted to clarify if the user was requesting service and asked for more details. The call ended abruptly after a short exchange without completing the conversation.
The user called Budget's after-hours assistant but the conversation was very brief and unclear, ending shortly after the initial greeting.
The user requested a callback regarding an estimate for a central air unit and provided their phone number for contact. The agent acknowledged the request before the user ended the call.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and no further interaction from the user before the call ended.

The user called the after-hours assistant and provided their location as Saint Louis, Missouri, but did not provide a ZIP code. The agent requested the ZIP code to check service coverage, but the call ended shortly after without further information.

The user, Jett, called to request that a quote for replacing their HVAC system be submitted to American Home Shield for coverage under their home warranty. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address, phone number, and preferred service days, and assured Jett that the request would be reviewed and followed up on the next business day. Jett declined information about maintenance programs but requested confirmation that the warranty submission was made.

Anthony called Budget's after-hours assistant to request moving his scheduled spring AC tune-up appointment from tomorrow to any available time today if possible. The agent confirmed the details and informed him that the team would reach out the next business day to confirm. Anthony declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, preferring to keep his original appointment if no same-day opening is available.

Frank McCoy called to schedule a regular air conditioner checkup through American Home Shield. The agent confirmed his preferred appointment time as anytime after 9 AM on Friday, verified his dispatch number and service address, and confirmed his callback number. The call concluded with the agent offering feedback options and Frank expressing satisfaction.

The user, Stacy, called customer service to report that a technician did not show up and requested immediate assistance. The agent documented the issue but could not connect to a live agent after hours and informed Stacy that a representative would call back on the next business day.

The user called back after missing a call and requested to be connected to the caller. The agent explained that call transfers are not possible after hours but offered to notify the team to return the call on the next business day. The call ended shortly after without further input from the user.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and the user hanging up immediately after. No further conversation or information exchange took place.
The user, Mike from Missouri Crane, called and requested to speak with Gigi. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to the appropriate person. The call ended with a successful transfer.

Jason from American Home Shield called to request confirmation of the last technician visit date for a combo heat and AC unit related to a recall request after a heat exchanger replacement. The agent confirmed the details and assured that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up with the authorizations team once the email is provided. Jason declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive confirmation of the call summary.

Marvin called to schedule a service appointment for an air conditioning issue covered by American Home Shield warranty. The agent confirmed the service details and address, noted the request for the earliest appointment, and then transferred the call to a live representative as requested by the user.
The user returned a call after receiving a message indicating there were questions. The agent acknowledged the callback and transferred the user to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief with only the agent greeting the user and no further conversation.

The user, Joan, called to schedule a furnace replacement under their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the dispatch number and appointment details, and the user declined additional maintenance program information. The user provided positive feedback about the agent's knowledge and the call concluded successfully.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call successfully to another representative.

The user called to confirm that a previously scheduled thermostat installation appointment was canceled and not needed anymore. The agent verified the user's details and confirmed the cancellation. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The agent attempted to engage the user by asking for their service address ZIP code, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.
The user called and asked to speak to the boss. The AI agent confirmed it was an AI and then transferred the call to a live agent as requested.

The user called to schedule routine seasonal maintenance for their air conditioner and furnace. The agent provided available time slots, and the user selected Tuesday, April 7th at 8:00 a.m. The appointment was provisionally booked, but the user requested an immediate update to their email address, which was not completed during the call.
The user called and requested to speak with CJ. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate person.

Mikisha Richardson called to schedule a free AC tune-up and cleaning through American Home Shield. She requested a morning appointment on April 10th, and the agent confirmed her details and will pass the information to the team for follow-up. Mikisha provided positive feedback, rating the experience a 5 and appreciating the communication.

Donna Sanchez called to schedule her yearly air conditioner maintenance. The agent provided available time slots, and Donna selected Monday, April 6th at 4:00 p.m., which was provisionally booked. The call concluded with the agent informing Donna about the dispatch fee and requesting feedback, which Donna declined.

The user called to request an estimate for a new Goodman HVAC system installation after a recent visit from Budget. The agent collected the user's contact information, service address, and preferences, and confirmed the details before informing the user that someone from Budget would reach out shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
